title: 3-letter words drill
description: Speed drill on common 3-letter words — pure muscle memory
text: all any but can did far for get had has her him his how its let lot may new now nor not now one our out put say see she sit ten the too try use was way who why yes

tips.text: A three-letter word lands as one motion, not three. Strike it whole.
Don't brake between the words. The flow is the point; keep it moving.

tips.target: all any but can did far for get had has her him his how its let lot may new now

tips.text: The space is a beat, not a rest. Count it in and the rhythm holds.

tips.target: nor not now one our out put say see she sit ten the too try use was way who why yes

character_tips.anna: Three-letter words are pure rhythm. Find the beat and stay on it.
character_tips.maxim: The workhorses of the language. Drill them until they need no thought at all.
character_tips.knopych: Small words, real speed. Steady taps, one after another.
character_tips.klavochka: Light and quick across the keys, like stones skipped over water.
